dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 573 RH: Studying NEPA’s Impact on Projects Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Yakym (for himself and Mr. Panetta) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources
Additional sponsors: Mr. Mann, Mr. Edwards, Mr. Fulcher, Mr. LaMalfa, and Mr. Gill of Texas
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on January 21, 2025

form.official-title: To require the Council on Environmental Quality to publish an annual report on environmental reviews and causes of action based on alleged non-compliance with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Studying NEPA’s Impact on Projects Act.

section.header: Annual report on NEPA’s impact on projects
section.text: Section 201 of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (42 U.S.C. 4341) is amended to read as follows:

section.header: Annual reports on NEPA’s impact on projects

subsection.text: Not later than July 1, 2026, and annually thereafter, the head of each lead agency shall submit to the Council on Environmental Quality a report on—

paragraph.text: each civil action alleging an agency action in violation of this Act that was active during the period beginning June 1 of the preceding year and ending June 1 of the current year, which shall identify—

subparagraph.text: with respect to each such civil action—

clause.text: the defendant lead agency, where applicable, and each plaintiff; and
the court in which the civil action was brought and any court to which a decision on the civil action was appealed;

subparagraph.text: the number of such civil actions, disaggregated by the defendant agency, with a single entry denoting the lead agency, where applicable, for cases involving multiple Federal agency defendants;
the alleged basis for each such civil action, disaggregated by covered sectors; and

the status and outcome, if applicable, of each civil action alleging an agency action in violation of this Act that was active during the period beginning June 1 of the preceding year and ending June 1 of the current year, including whether—

clause.text: the civil action resulted in a decision to hold unlawful and set aside the agency action within the meaning of section 706(2) of title 5, United States Code;
the civil action resulted in a decision allowing the Federal agency to proceed with the major Federal action;
the civil action resulted in an order remanding the matter to the agency for reconsideration without vacating the analysis;
any claim under the civil action was resolved by a settlement agreement or consent decree between any plaintiff and the defendant lead agency;
the civil action is still active; and

any of the plaintiffs received an award, including an award of costs pursuant to section 2412 of title 28, United States Code, except that if such information is received after the lead agency submits the applicable report to the Council on Environmental Quality, the lead agency shall include such information in the subsequent report;

paragraph.text: the length of environmental impact statements made publicly available pursuant to section 102(2)(C) during the period of 5 years that ends on June 1 of the current year, which shall include—

subparagraph.text: the average and median page count of all such environmental impact statements (not including the page count of any citations or appendices), including such page counts disaggregated by quartiles;
the average and median page count of any citations and appendices included in such environmental impact statements;
the number of such environmental impact statements, disaggregated by lead agency and subagency as applicable;

a description of trends in average and median page count of such environmental impact statements and any citations and appendices included in such environmental impact statements compared to prior reports published by the Council on Environmental Quality; and
for each report submitted under this section through 2028, a disaggregation of the data from before and after the date of the enactment of the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023;

paragraph.text: an estimate of the cost to prepare the environmental impact statements described in paragraph (2), including—

subparagraph.text: the full-time equivalent personnel hour costs, contractor costs, and other direct costs of the lead agency that prepared the environmental impact statement; and

if practicable, and noted where not practicable, the costs incurred by cooperating agencies, participating agencies, any project sponsor that prepared an environmental impact statement under the supervision of the lead agency, and contractors; and

paragraph.text: the timelines to complete environmental impact statements pursuant to section 102(2)(C) during the period of 10 years that ends on June 1 of the current year, which shall include—

subparagraph.text: with respect to each major Federal action commenced during such period of 10 years, the date on which (as applicable)—

clause.text: the project sponsor submitted an application for any permit or other authorization for the project;
the lead agency began the scoping;
the notice of intent to prepare the environmental impact statement was made publicly available;
the environmental impact statement was made publicly available;
the record of decision was made publicly available; and
the lead agency provided to the project sponsor notice to proceed on the project;

subparagraph.text: the average and median completion timelines during such period of 10 years for each document described in subparagraph (A);
a description of trends in completion timelines during such period of 10 years for such documents compared to prior reports published by the Council on Environmental Quality; and
for each report submitted under this section through 2033, a disaggregation of the data from before and after the date of the enactment of the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023.

subsection.header: Publication and submission of reports by the Council on Environmental Quality

paragraph.text: The Council on Environmental Qualify shall annually—

subparagraph.text: publish the reports received under subsection (a) on the website of the Council on Environmental Quality; and
submit such reports to the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate.

paragraph.text: The Council on Environmental Quality may carry out paragraph (1)(B) by including the reports received under subsection (a) in the report submitted under section 107(h).

The Council on Environmental Quality shall publish with each report published under paragraph (1)(A) the underlying data used to prepare each such report and include any citations or other information necessary for the public to locate records related to the court proceedings for any civil action described in subsection (a)(1).

subsection.text: To the extent practicable, the information included in each report submitted to the Council on Environmental Quality under subsection (a) shall be disaggregated by covered sector.
In this section, the term covered sector means any of the following sectors:

paragraph.text: Any other sector as determined by the Council on Environmental Quality or lead agency.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
